classified_blocks.source_text_excerpt: Unit covering rational and irrational numbers, density proofs, decimal representations, square root spiral.
Basic introduction and definition of rational numbers within the number system unit.

classified_blocks.source_heading: Representation of rational numbers on the number line
classified_blocks.source_text_excerpt: Methods and techniques for plotting rational numbers accurately on a linear scale.

classified_blocks.source_heading: Density of rational numbers and its proof
classified_blocks.source_text_excerpt: Mathematical proofs demonstrating the density property between any two rational numbers.

classified_blocks.source_heading: Finding rational numbers between any two rational numbers
classified_blocks.source_text_excerpt: Procedural methods to identify intermediate rational values within a given interval.
Conversion and classification of rational numbers into terminating or recurring decimals.
Definition and characteristics distinguishing irrational numbers from rational ones.
Rigorous mathematical proofs establishing the non-rational nature of square roots.
Geometric construction illustrating the visualization of irrational numbers.
Unit covering polynomials, algebraic identities, sequences, and linear equations.
Study of arithmetic and geometric progressions including nth term formulas.
Derivation and application of standard algebraic identities with geometric models.
Graphical and algebraic methods for solving systems of linear equations.
Unit covering history, Cartesian system, distance formula, and midpoint calculations.
Historical context and development of the Cartesian plane system.
Fundamentals of axes, quadrants, and plotting points in a two-dimensional plane.

classified_blocks.source_heading: Distance between two points in the 2-D plane
classified_blocks.source_text_excerpt: Derivation and application of the distance formula for coordinate pairs.
Calculating the center point between two coordinates and related geometric properties.
Unit covering Euclid's axioms, lines, angles, triangles, quadrilaterals, and circles.

classified_blocks.source_heading: Introduction to Euclid's Geometry: Axioms and Postulates
classified_blocks.source_text_excerpt: Foundational definitions, axioms, postulates, and historical context of geometry.
Properties of intersecting lines, parallelism, linear pairs, and vertically opposite angles.
Conditions for triangle congruence including SAS, SSS, ASA, RHS, and AAS.
Theorems regarding parallelograms, midpoints, symmetry, and properties of four-sided figures.
Definitions of chords, arcs, sectors; cyclic quadrilaterals and angle theorems.
Unit covering perimeter, area formulas including Heron's and Brahmagupta's, surface areas.
Formulas for perimeter of shapes, circumference, arc length, and area calculations.
Calculations for surface areas and volumes of various solids like cubes, cylinders, cones.
Unit covering data representation, central tendency measures, probability concepts.
Methods for collecting, organizing, and displaying data using stacked bar graphs.
Computation and interpretation of mean, median, mode, and weighted averages.
Theoretical vs empirical probability, sample spaces, events, tree diagrams.

formative_content.title: Continuous and Holistic Assessment
formative_content.source_text: Continuous and holistic assessment through projects, activities, and investigations forms an integral part of the learning process, moving beyond summative examinations.

excluded_content: None explicitly excluded in the provided text; all listed topics are part of the curriculum.
Note on SSA congruence condition: 'Explain why SSA is not, in general, a valid congruence condition' implies it is non-assessed as a proof method but assessed for understanding its limitations.
teacher_notes: Curriculum redesigned in alignment with NEP 2020 and NCF-SE 2023.
Prioritize deep conceptual understanding, logical reasoning, and competency-based education over rote learning.
Integrate Indian Knowledge System (e.g., Baudhayana's Sulbasutras, Brahmagupta, Aryabhata) with contemporary knowledge.
Emphasis on computational thinking: decomposing problems, algorithmic problem-solving, visualisation.
Use of real-life applications and experiential learning is critical.
Assessment includes continuous holistic assessment via projects, activities, and investigations.

Prescribed Books for Lab Practical: 1. Mathematics - Textbook for class IX (NCERT), 2. Guidelines for Mathematics Laboratory in Schools (CBSE), 3. Laboratory Manual - Mathematics (NCERT), 4. Mathematics exemplar problems (NCERT).
Time allocation for Question Paper Design: 3 Hours.
Internal Assessment components: Pen Paper Test and Multiple Assessment (10 Marks), Portfolio (5 Marks), Lab Practical (5 Marks).
